item.title: Commonwealth Fusion Systems leans on magnets for near-term revenue
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Realta Fusion is buying magnets from Commonwealth Fusion Systems, providing a revenue stopgap.

item.title: Diverse teams start with diverse VCs
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: It is the path of least resistance for a growth-stage company to hire from the familiar Silicon Valley pipelines but if a founder wants a diverse team, that value has to be put into practice from the very first hire.

item.title: The reputation of troubled YC startup Delve has gotten even worse
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Delve faces new allegations that it violated the open source license of its customer, Sim.ai, by taking the customers's tool and passing it off as its own.

item.title: Startup funding shatters all records in Q1
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The record quarterly fundraise largely fueled four mega-deals into OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Waymo. But it also indicates a generally hot market.

item.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

item.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The AI recruiting startup confirmed a security incident after an extortion hacking crew took credit for stealing data from the company's systems.

item.title: Toyota’s Woven Capital appoints new CIO and COO in push for finding the ‘future of mobility’
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Woven Capital is the growth-stage venture capital arm of Toyota, focused on backing founders building in space, cybersecurity, and autonomous driving.

item.title: It’s not your imagination: AI seed startups are commanding higher valuations
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Among the most recent Y Combinator cohort, many startups were commanding $40 million valuations. But with more money comes higher expectations.

item.title: Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto’s Chris Dixon
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Less than a year after launching, with checks from some of the biggest names in Silicon Valley, crowdsourced AI model feedback startup Yupp is closing its business, the company said Tuesday.

item.title: Whoop’s valuation just tripled to $10 billion
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The fitness tracking startup just closed a $575 million Series G with Cristiano Ronaldo and LeBron James among its investors. The obvious question looming over a round of this size at this valuation: Is an IPO coming?

item.title: Nomadic raises $8.4 million to wrangle the data pouring off autonomous vehicles
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company turns footage from robots into structured, searchable datasets with a deep learning model.

item.title: The company behind ClassPass and Mindbody just got a lot bigger with a $7.5B merger
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The merger is a sign that the fitness industry is continuing to move toward consolidation to compete at a larger scale. Recent moves include MyFitnessPal acquiring Cal AI, an AI calorie counting app, and Strava buying two apps: cycling app The Breakaway and running app Runna.

item.title: Exclusive: Runway launches $10M fund, Builders program to support early-stage AI startups
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Runway is launching a $10 million fund and startup program to back companies building with its AI video models, as it pushes toward interactive, real-time “video intelligence” applications.

item.title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: LiteLLM had obtained two security compliance certifications via Delve and fell victim to some horrific credential-stealing malware last week.

item.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026 and how to put your best application forward
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: Here's what we're looking for in your Startup Battlefield 200 application and how to make sure your application reflects it.

item.title: Delve whistleblower strikes again, with alleged receipts about ‘fake compliance’
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Shortly after Delve's founder published a lengthy denial of allegations, while promising changes, the accuser came forward with more alleged details.
